conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who's 8 years old, and Blake, who's 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me ask you some questions about each child to determine their eligibility.

Let's start with **Alex, who is 8 years old**:

**Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me here in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great, thank you. And just to confirm for Alex: **Are you responsible for Alex's care (meaning you provide for their needs like food, clothes, etc.)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of all of Alex's needs - food, clothes, everything.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now let me ask about **Blake, who is 20 years old**:

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** (For example, attending college, university, or an approved training course)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?** (This won't affect your eligibility, but it may affect whether you need to pay a tax charge on the Child Benefit you receive)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

**Good news!** You **are eligible** for Child Benefit for **Alex**.

**However**, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for **Blake**.

### Here's the breakdown:

**For Alex (8 years old):** ✅ **Eligible**
- Alex is under 16 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's care and provide for all their needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

**For Blake (20 years old):** ❌ **Not Eligible**
- Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training
- Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training
- However, Child Benefit does not extend beyond age 20, even if they are in education or training

### What this means:
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex. Since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you won't need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit for Alex. If Blake was previously covered and has now turned 20, your Child Benefit for Blake would have stopped.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for their care by providing for all their needs including food and clothes. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 only continues if they remain in approved education or training, and this does not extend beyond age 20 regardless of education status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (8 years old) and Blake (20 years old). Alex meets all the eligibility criteria as a child under 16 living with the claimant in the UK, with the claimant being responsible for their care. Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria because, although children can qualify up to age 20 if in approved education or training, Blake is 20 and not in any education or training. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex only.
